contract.name: v2/download/awards.md
contract.description: Starts an awards download job and returns absolute links for polling status and retrieving the generated ZIP.

columns.description: Optional list of column identifiers to request in the export.
columns.constraints: Optional; array of strings. Server echoes unknown column names without validation.

filters.description: Search filters determining which awards are exported, following the Search Filters v2 structures (agencies, keywords, time_period, etc.).
filters.constraints: Required; must be a non-empty object. Valid combinations are flexible, but the API silently drops unrecognized keys and injects a comprehensive award_type_codes list when absent. Specific nested validations apply (e.g., time_period arrays must contain at least one entry, agency objects require type and tier, recipient_search_text must be an array).

file_format.description: Controls the generated file format for each dataset in the download.
file_format.constraints: Optional; defaults to 'csv'. Observed valid values are 'csv', 'tsv', and 'pstxt'. Other values are rejected with HTTP 400.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to include in the generated files.
limit.constraints: Optional; defaults to 500000. Values above 500000 return HTTP 422. Zero or negative numbers are accepted without server-side enforcement, so clients should guard against unintended results.

status_url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL for polling the download job's status.

file_url.description: Absolute HTTPS URL for the generated ZIP once ready.

download_request.description: Echo of the processed request, including server defaults such as download_types=['elasticsearch_awards','elasticsearch_sub_awards'], injected award_type_codes, and the effective filters, limit, file_format, columns, and request_type.

body.detail: At least one filter is required.

quirks: download_request.download_types are always ['elasticsearch_awards', 'elasticsearch_sub_awards'] regardless of requested data set labels.
The API injects a comprehensive award_type_codes list into download_request.filters even when the client omits it.
transaction_keyword_search inputs are accepted in the request but omitted from download_request.filters.
file_url values are always absolute HTTPS URLs, not relative paths as shown in documentation examples.
filters must be present and non-empty, yet any unrecognized keys inside filters are silently dropped rather than producing validation errors.
columns are not validated server-side; unknown column identifiers are echoed back.
risks: API connections over IPv6 have returned empty replies; callers should force IPv4 or retry with alternate DNS resolution.
Silently dropping unknown filter keys can lead to broader downloads than intended if typos go unnoticed.
Lack of column name validation may produce downstream processing errors when typos slip through.
The default limit of 500000 combined with injected award_type_codes can create very large exports unless callers actively constrain filters and limits.
gaps: Impact of zero or negative limit values on the generated dataset remains unverified because resulting files were not downloaded.
Download job lifecycle beyond the initial POST (status polling and file retrieval) was not validated.
Exact server-side handling of unfamiliar filter structures is unclear beyond the observed silent drops.

probes.notes: pass – filters can omit time_period entirely; server still injects award_type_codes and defaults.
fail – time_period must contain at least one object when present.
fail – filters field must be a non-empty object.
pass – custom columns, TSV format, and small limit were honored.
fail – invalid file_format rejected with explicit enum list.
pass – recipient_search_text accepts multi-value arrays despite documentation limits.
fail – server enforces an upper limit of 500000.

mismatches: Documentation lists download_types as ['awards', 'sub_awards'], but responses return ['elasticsearch_awards', 'elasticsearch_sub_awards'].
Documentation depicts file_url as a relative path, yet production responses return absolute HTTPS URLs.
Documentation constrains recipient_search_text to a single value, while the API accepts multi-entry arrays.
Documentation treats transaction_keyword_search as a persisted filter, but the API drops it from download_request.filters.
gaps: Impact of zero or negative limit values on the generated dataset remains unverified because resulting files were not downloaded.
Download job lifecycle beyond the initial POST (status polling and file retrieval) was not validated.
Exact server-side handling of unfamiliar filter structures is unclear beyond the observed silent drops.
risks: API connections over IPv6 have returned empty replies; callers should force IPv4 or retry with alternate DNS resolution.
Silently dropping unknown filter keys can lead to broader downloads than intended if typos go unnoticed.
Lack of column name validation may produce downstream processing errors when typos slip through.
The default limit of 500000 combined with injected award_type_codes can create very large exports unless callers actively constrain filters and limits.
